output:
**(B)**.

The structural pattern is the same in all three: **demonstrated competence at level N is treated as evidence of competence at level N+1, but the two levels demand fundamentally different capabilities.** The promotion criterion (technical excellence) is orthogonal to the new role's requirements (management, coordination, coaching), so the system reliably converts high performers into poor ones while simultaneously removing them from where they added value.

(A), (C), and (D) each describe a surface-level takeaway or remedy, but none of them identify the *structural* mechanism — the category error in the selection function itself.
